title: All-measured Llama7B attention L2 clustered schedule
hypothesis: Replacing the remaining local L1 attention abstractions with measured compute, full-value tile, exact softmax generator, and local NoC costs will show whether the prior full-value clustered frontier still favors fp16_nm1 or shifts once the local datapath stack is measured.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the all-measured L1 cost stack change the Llama7B 131k native-GQA KV8 clustered attention frontier relative to the prior full-value clustered schedule?
include: measured compute PPA, full-value attention tile datapaths, exact-int softmax weight generator PPA, and FIFO/router local NoC anchors
Llama7B 131k native-GQA KV8 clustered attention schedule ranking
exclude: wider nm and cluster-count sweeps beyond the measured nm1/nm2 anchors
follow_on_broad_sweep: sweep larger compute parallelism after the local all-measured baseline is stable
replace analytic SRAM and global NoC terms with physically constrained measured blocks

expected_benefit: reduce the number of abstracted datapath components in the Llama7B frontier estimate
quantify whether exact softmax generator and local NoC costs materially change the current frontier ranking
record the residual SRAM/global-NoC assumptions explicitly before larger die-size exploration
risks: SRAM service and global NoC arbitration remain analytic and can dominate long-context Llama7B behavior
the campaign harness still uses the MLP smoke reuse path for compute scheduling artifacts
only nm1 and nm2 measured compute anchors are ranked in this retry

expected_result.reason: This retry should complete without retaining millions of estimator rows and should show whether measured softmax generator and local NoC costs change the full-value clustered frontier.
